Name: mixed_precision_optimization
Title: Optimizing GPU Memory Bandwidth for Mixed Precision Deep Learning
Short Hypothesis: By optimizing GPU memory bandwidth for mixed precision deep learning, we can achieve significant improvements in model performance and efficiency. We hypothesize that a combination of novel memory access patterns, data compression techniques, and precision-aware scheduling can reduce memory bandwidth usage while maintaining model accuracy.
Related Work: Recent works such as AMP-ViT, SNIP, and EMQ have explored mixed precision quantization and training techniques for deep learning models. However, these works focus primarily on optimizing model weights and activations, whereas our proposal focuses on optimizing GPU memory bandwidth, which is a critical bottleneck in modern deep learning systems. Our approach is distinct from existing works in that it targets the memory hierarchy and aims to reduce memory bandwidth usage through a combination of hardware-aware optimizations.
Abstract: Deep learning models are increasingly computationally intensive and memory-bound, making it essential to optimize GPU memory bandwidth for improved performance. This proposal introduces a novel approach to optimizing GPU memory bandwidth for mixed precision deep learning. Our method combines novel memory access patterns, data compression techniques, and precision-aware scheduling to reduce memory bandwidth usage while maintaining model accuracy. We evaluate our approach on a range of deep learning benchmarks and demonstrate significant improvements in model performance and efficiency.
Experiments: We will conduct experiments on a range of deep learning benchmarks, including image classification, language modeling, and object detection. We will evaluate our approach using metrics such as model accuracy, memory bandwidth usage, and execution time. We will also compare our approach to existing state-of-the-art methods for mixed precision quantization and training.
Risk Factors and Limitations: One potential risk factor is that our approach may not be effective for all types of deep learning models or workloads. Additionally, our approach may require significant modifications to existing deep learning frameworks and hardware, which could be a limitation. However, we believe that the potential benefits of our approach make it worth exploring, and we plan to mitigate these risks through careful experimentation and evaluation.
